steps.name: Remove auto-start timer from useSessionLogger
steps.criteria: logSet completes without starting rest timer; useTimerStore not imported in useSessionLogger; timerStore.start not called in useSessionLogger; unit test confirms timer store unchanged after logSet
steps.implementation_notes: Remove timerStore.start(DEFAULT_REST_DURATION_MS), const timerStore = useTimerStore(), useTimerStore import, and DEFAULT_REST_DURATION_MS from useSessionLogger.ts.

steps.name: Make RestTimer always visible with idle state
steps.criteria: RestTimer renders idle state when isRunning is false; idle state shows Start Rest button calling start(); running state shows countdown and controls; sticky variant works in both states; unit test covers idle and running paths
steps.implementation_notes: Remove 'if (!isRunning) return null'. Add idle branch with Start Rest button using start() from useRestTimer. Add timer--idle CSS class. Add padding-bottom to .session so sticky timer does not overlap content.

steps.name: Add set counter and timer context label
steps.criteria: Idle state shows next set number e.g. Set 1; running state shows rest after set N; set counter increments after logSet; setNumber prop optional; unit test verifies counter text
steps.implementation_notes: Add optional setNumber prop to RestTimer. In SessionScreen pass setNumber={entryCount}. Idle: 'Set N+1'. Running: 'Rest after set N'. Show as timer__label element.
